name: North La Guardia Lagoon Trail
name_original: Laguna La Guardia – running circular por el Camino Norte de la Laguna
description: Jog the moderate 7.5-mile North La Guardia Lagoon Trail, gaining 548 feet in 1 hour 22 minutes, with views of the lagoon and gypsum hills.
region_intro: Jogging routes around La Guardia are characterized by a landscape featuring lagoons, historical castles, and open countryside. The region offers a mix of relatively flat paths around water bodies and routes with moderate elevation gains, providing varied running experiences. Trails often traverse agricultural areas and natural spaces, with some routes leading to points of interest like ancient castles or hermitages. The terrain is generally accessible, suitable for moderate-level running. Best jogging routes around La Guardia The most popular jogging route is Laguna del Longar – running circular from Lillo, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 2 minutes to complete. This circular route offers views of the Laguna del Longar with minimal elevation changes. Another top favourite among local runners is Laguna La Guardia – running circular via the North Path of the Laguna, a moderate 7.5 miles (12.1 km) path. This route provides a longer run around the Laguna, featuring some rolling terrain and scenic views. Local runners also love the Castillo de Almonacid loop from Almonacid de Toledo, a 3.3 miles (5.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain towards the historic Castillo de Almonacid, often completed in about 38 minutes. Jogging around La Guardia is defined by routes circling lagoons, paths leading to historical structures like castles, and trails through open countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, primarily focusing on moderate-difficulty running. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than a dozen runners have used komoot to explore La Guardia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes around La Guardia?
A: The jogging routes around La Guardia are characterized by a mix of relatively flat paths around water bodies, such as lagoons, and routes with moderate elevation gains. You'll find trails traversing agricultural areas and natural spaces, with some leading to historical points of interest like ancient castles. The terrain is generally accessible and suitable for moderate-level running.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the La Guardia area?
A: Yes, several popular running routes in La Guardia are circular. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021494201/laguna-del-longar-running-circular-from-lillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Longar – running circular from Lillo&lt;/a&gt; is a 5.8-mile trail with minimal elevation changes, offering views of the lagoon. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021461287/laguna-la-guardia-running-circular-via-the-north-path-of-the-laguna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna La Guardia – running circular via the North Path of the Laguna&lt;/a&gt;, a longer 7.5-mile path with some rolling terrain.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks I can see while jogging in La Guardia?
A: Jogging routes in La Guardia often feature scenic views of lagoons and open countryside. You can also encounter historical structures. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38148528" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Almonacid loop from Almonacid de Toledo&lt;/a&gt; leads you towards the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2127963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almonacid Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable attractions in the wider area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5445505" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Tembleque&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6313232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción (Tembleque)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in La Guardia?
A: While all listed routes are rated as moderate, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021494201/laguna-del-longar-running-circular-from-lillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Longar – running circular from Lillo&lt;/a&gt; is a good option for those seeking a less strenuous run, as it features minimal elevation changes around the lagoon. Its 5.8-mile distance makes it manageable for many runners.

Q: How many running trails are available in the La Guardia region?
A: There are several dedicated jogging routes available in the La Guardia region, with this guide highlighting 4 specific trails. These routes offer varied experiences, from lagoon-side runs to paths leading to historical sites.

Q: Can I find longer running routes for endurance training in La Guardia?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a longer run, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021461287/laguna-la-guardia-running-circular-via-the-north-path-of-the-laguna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna La Guardia – running circular via the North Path of the Laguna&lt;/a&gt; is a great choice. It's a 7.5-mile (12.1 km) path that offers a longer run around the Laguna with some rolling terrain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40009763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Monroy loop from Lillo&lt;/a&gt;, which is also around 7.5 miles (12.1 km).

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by historical sites or castles?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38148528" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Almonacid loop from Almonacid de Toledo&lt;/a&gt; is a 3.3-mile trail that takes you through varied terrain towards the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2127963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almonacid Castle&lt;/a&gt;, offering a unique blend of exercise and history.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the running trails?
A: The running trails in La Guardia are often set amidst natural spaces, featuring lagoons and open countryside. You'll experience a landscape characterized by water bodies and agricultural areas, providing a tranquil and scenic backdrop for your run.

Q: What is a popular running route that offers views of a lagoon?
A: A highly popular running route that provides excellent lagoon views is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021494201/laguna-del-longar-running-circular-from-lillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Longar – running circular from Lillo&lt;/a&gt;. This 5.8-mile circular trail allows you to enjoy the serene Laguna del Longar with minimal elevation changes.

Q: Are the running routes around La Guardia suitable for different fitness levels?
A: The jogging routes in La Guardia are primarily of moderate difficulty, offering options for various ability levels. While there are no routes classified as easy or difficult in this guide, the moderate trails provide a good balance for most runners, with some featuring minimal elevation and others offering more rolling terrain.

Q: What is the average duration for completing a running route in La Guardia?
A: The duration for completing a running route in La Guardia varies depending on the trail's length and your pace. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021494201/laguna-del-longar-running-circular-from-lillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Longar – running circular from Lillo&lt;/a&gt; typically takes about 1 hour and 2 minutes, while the shorter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38148528" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Almonacid loop from Almonacid de Toledo&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 38 minutes.
tips: Beautiful views that can be seen from the top where the Santo Niño hermitage is located. The panoramic views of the Cedrón stream next to the La Guardia lagoon are worthy of a postcard.

The Mirador de la Laguna in La Guardia, Toledo, offers a spectacular view of the Laguna de La Guardia, a wetland that has been remodeled to promote biodiversity. This viewing point is an excellent spot for bird watching, including endangered species such as the white-headed duck.

faqs.question: What is the North La Guardia Lagoon Trail?
faqs.answer: The "North La Guardia Lagoon Trail" on komoot is a jogging route that primarily utilizes paths within Pugsley Creek Park and Flushing Meadows Corona Park near LaGuardia Airport. These parks offer diverse environments for runners with water views and trail-like experiences.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for jogging on this route?
faqs.answer: The route combines varied terrain. In Pugsley Creek Park, you'll find a wide multi-use path, including a boardwalk section over the creek, generally considered easy. Flushing Meadows Corona Park features immaculately paved pathways around its lakes, with some sections like the Willow Lake trail offering a half-mile wood chip surface. Overall, the route is considered moderate for jogging.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is generally accessible for beginners, especially if you focus on shorter sections within Pugsley Creek Park or Flushing Meadows Corona Park. The paths are wide and mostly flat, though the full 12 km distance and moderate difficulty might be a good challenge for those new to jogging longer distances.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: For Pugsley Creek Park, street parking is typically available in the surrounding residential areas. For Flushing Meadows Corona Park, there are several parking lots available, particularly near attractions like the Unisphere or Citi Field, which provide good access to the park's extensive path network.

faqs.question: Is the route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Pugsley Creek Park and Flushing Meadows Corona Park generally allow dogs on leash. Please ensure your dog is on a leash at all times and that you clean up after them to maintain the cleanliness of the parks for all users.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and fall offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging, with blooming foliage in spring and vibrant colors in autumn. Summer can be hot and humid, while winter might present icy conditions on paths. The parks offer unique scenery year-round, but consider weather conditions for comfort and safety.

faqs.question: What can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll experience diverse scenery. Highlights include Views of the gypsum hills of La Guardia and the serene Laguna La Guardia. In Pugsley Creek Park, you'll see salt marshes, maritime forest, and views of the Whitestone and Throgs Neck bridges, and even LaGuardia Airport. Flushing Meadows Corona Park offers views of the Unisphere, Citi Field, and Arthur Ashe Stadium, alongside its two main lakes, Meadow Lake and Willow Lake.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access Pugsley Creek Park or Flushing Meadows Corona Park, making the route freely accessible for jogging.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route primarily follows sections of the Camino Norte de la Laguna-La Guardia, which makes up 88% of the path. Within Flushing Meadows Corona Park, it connects to the extensive network of paved pathways that crisscross the park.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: Both parks are rich in wildlife. Pugsley Creek Park is known for its diverse bird species and wetland wildlife. Flushing Meadows Corona Park, particularly around Willow Lake, is a protected area for migratory birds and various plant life, offering excellent opportunities for birdwatching.

faqs.question: Is the route a loop or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: The "North La Guardia Lagoon Trail" as mapped on komoot is designed as a circular route, allowing you to start and end at the same point without retracing your steps.

waypoints.name: Views of the gypsum hills of La Guardia
